behavior_summary: DTABLE::look is a short event handler that runs when an actor 'looks' at the table object. It prepares per-viewer state (sets info and applies a process-exclude) and then causes the table item to 'bark' the short description "table " to the viewing process.
The handler allocates a transient string buffer for the literal "table ", calls Item::bark with that string, the viewer/process id, and the implicit item context; it then frees the buffer.

The return value from Item::bark is captured and passed through a boolean/implication sequence (implies + pop) before the script suspends and returns, suggesting the bark result influences event propagation or suppression.
notable_control_flow: Entry triggered by the 'U8P_DTABLE::look' event (an area/inspect trigger).
A 'set info' followed by a 'process exclude' is applied before emitting output — likely to avoid self-feedback or to mark per-viewer state.
After calling Item::bark the code inspects the call's return (push/implie/pop) and then suspends; the imply/pop sequence is an engine-level boolean propagation step rather than complex branching.
open_questions: What exact info key is being set by the 'set info' opcode and how it affects per-viewer behavior (the disassembly shows a set-info operation but the numeric id/semantic meaning is not explicit).
What precise semantics does the process-exclude have in this context (preventing callbacks to the viewing process, blocking re-entrant events, or something else)?
What does the return value from Item::bark represent here and how does the implies/pop sequence affect higher-level event handling?

main_function.name: U8P_DTABLE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle a player's or actor's 'U8P_DTABLE::look' (examine) action on the table: prepare per-viewer state and emit the short description "table " to the viewer via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the 'U8P_DTABLE::look' verb/trigger (player or actor inspects/looks at the table).

function_map.name: U8P_DTABLE::look
function_map.role: Look handler for the table object; prepares per-viewer info/exclusion and emits the short description to the viewing process.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to display or emit a short text message (here: "table ") toward a specific process/player.

external_calls.purpose: Make the table item emit/display the literal string "table " to the target process/viewer passed into the U8P_DTABLE::look event; used to present the item's short description.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: DTABLE::look is a short event handler that runs when an actor 'looks' at the table object. It prepares per-viewer state (sets info and applies a process-exclude) and then causes the table item to 'bark' the short description "table " to the viewing process.
